posts.title: ocabra
posts.excerpt: Vocabra transforms your daily phone usage into a natural language-learning experience through passive immersion, lock screen vocabulary, and smart repetition. Learn new words effortlessly throughout the day without stressful study sessions or overwhelming lessons.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
*Learning a language has always had one major problem: consistency.
*
Most people start motivated, download a few apps, study for several days, and then slowly stop opening them. Traditional language learning often feels like homework — repetitive exercises, endless memorization, and long study sessions that are difficult to maintain in everyday life.

That is exactly why Vocabra was created.

Vocabra takes a different approach to language learning. Instead of forcing intensive studying, it transforms your phone into a passive learning environment where vocabulary becomes part of your daily routine naturally.

## The Idea Behind Vocabra

The concept is simple:

You already look at your phone hundreds of times per day.
So why not turn those moments into small language-learning opportunities?

Vocabra uses:

- Lock screen vocabulary
- Widgets
- Passive repetition
- Visual associations
- Daily immersion techniques

Instead of sitting down for a “study session,” users absorb words gradually throughout the day.

### **This mirrors how people naturally acquire language exposure in real life: through repetition, context, and constant contact with words over time.**

## Designed for Real Life

One of the biggest goals behind Vocabra was to make language learning feel lightweight and non-intrusive.

The app is designed for:

- Busy people
- Students
- Travelers
- Casual learners
- People who struggle with consistency

Even a few seconds of exposure multiple times a day can significantly improve vocabulary retention over time.

Rather than overwhelming users with grammar-heavy lessons immediately, Vocabra focuses first on building familiarity and comfort with the language.

## Current Languages

Vocabra currently supports:

Ukranian
English
Spanish
Hebrew

More languages and learning systems are planned for future updates.

A Different Philosophy of Learning

Most language apps are built around active study.

Vocabra is built around environmental learning.

The idea is not to replace traditional study entirely, but to complement it by creating a constant background layer of exposure. Over time, words stop feeling unfamiliar because users repeatedly encounter them naturally during everyday phone usage.

This approach is inspired by:

Passive memory reinforcement
Immersion learning
Habit psychology
Micro-learning systems
Built Independently

Vocabra was designed and developed independently as a personal project focused on creating a calmer, more natural learning experience.

From the UI design to the learning flow, the goal was to avoid the stress and pressure that often comes with productivity-focused educational apps.

## The focus was simplicity:

- Open your phone
- See words naturally
- Learn gradually
- Stay consistent without effort
- Available Now

Vocabra is now officially available on the App Store. [DOWNLOAD HERE ](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/vocabra-learn-languages/id6769436509)

For anyone interested in learning languages in a more passive, modern, and low-pressure way, this is just the beginning.

More updates, features, languages, and experiments are already in development.

posts.title: Cheervinsky - Designing Control in the Era of AI Overload
posts.excerpt: Cheervinsky started from a personal need to regain control over attention in a noisy digital world. Beginning with Calmloop for breathing and focus, it has grown into a set of tools that help reduce distraction, manage screen usage, and restore clarity in the age of constant information and AI-generated content.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
Cheervinsky started with a single, very practical problem - a problem of one person trying to handle the changes of today’s world.

At some point, the relationship with our phones quietly shifted. It is no longer just a tool - it has become a constant source of noise. Endless scrolling, fragmented attention, and an overwhelming amount of content, much of it AI-generated, repetitive, or simply lacking real value. The signal started to disappear.

What began as a personal attempt to regain control evolved into the first solution -###  [Calmloop](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cheervinsky.calmloop). It focuses on something fundamental: stabilizing the mind. Through breathing techniques, EMDR-inspired patterns, and concentration-based meditation, it is designed to reduce mental overload and bring attention back to a steady baseline.

But solving one layer of the problem exposed another.

It wasn’t just about calming the mind - it became clear that the broader system of phone usage itself needed to be understood and reshaped. That realization led to expanding the idea into a wider set of tools. [Screen control became a core focus](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cheervinsky.screentimemonitor) - not only by structuring or limiting usage, but by going deeper and even measuring how different usage patterns influence mood and mental state.

From there, Cheervinsky naturally evolved into a broader ecosystem.

Some apps explore mindset and internal narratives, such as **[Neurodream](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cheervinsky.neurodreamapp)**, approaching concepts similar to the law of attraction in a more structured and intentional way. Others focus on cognition and mental engagement from a different angle - games like [Reverse Audio Game](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cheervinsky.reverseaudiogame), [Chess](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.itmaryna.chess), [Emoji-based memory training](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.cheervinsky.memorygame), and tile-matching systems are not just entertainment, but small, practical exercises in attention, perception, and mental flexibility.

{{image:data/media/wedqeww-moq4e1rh-04m58.jpg|Calmloop |size=medium|align=left|wrap=true|shadow=true}}

{{image:data/media/reverse_audio-moq4e1s9-46qjr.png|Reverse Audio Game|size=medium|align=right|wrap=true|shadow=false}}

### At the same time, the ecosystem leaves room for lightness. Even something as simple and absurd as a farting app has its place - because maintaining control also means allowing moments of release, humor, and emotional balance.

Alongside these, Cheervinsky includes practical tools - everyday utilities like calculators - reinforcing the idea that clarity and simplicity should exist even in the smallest daily interactions.

What started as a response to a single personal need has grown into a collection of products with a shared direction: helping people navigate the cognitive and emotional challenges of modern life.

In an era where AI accelerates content creation and information becomes increasingly abundant - and often less reliable - the real challenge is no longer access, but discernment. Attention becomes a resource. Clarity becomes a skill.

### Cheervinsky is built around that idea.

Not to fight technology, but to rebalance it. Not to remove complexity, but to make it manageable. To create tools that do not demand more from you - but help you take something back.

posts.title: Box Breathing by Calmloop
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
Box breathing is one of those deceptively simple techniques that feels almost too basic to matter—until you actually try it. Matching your inhale and exhale to equal durations creates a steady rhythm that directly influences your nervous system, helping shift you out of stress and into a more controlled, focused state. It’s not just a wellness trend; it’s a method used in high-pressure environments because it works.


The structure is straightforward: inhale for a set number of seconds, exhale for the same amount, and repeat. That symmetry is the key. When your breath becomes consistent and predictable, your body follows—heart rate stabilizes, mental noise drops, and you regain a sense of control that’s often lost in fast, fragmented daily routines.

This is exactly where Calmloop fits in. Instead of manually counting or trying to maintain the rhythm in your head, the app guides you through the process. You can adjust the duration of each phase—shorter cycles for quick resets, longer ones for deeper relaxation—so it adapts to how you feel in the moment, not the other way around.

What makes it especially effective is pairing the breathing with sound. Whether it’s ambient noise, subtle textures, or calming environments, syncing your breath with audio creates a more immersive experience. It’s easier to stay consistent, easier to focus, and frankly, it just feels better.

Box breathing on its own is powerful. With the right pacing and sound, it becomes something you actually want to come back to—not just when you’re overwhelmed, but as a daily baseline for staying clear and steady.

posts.title: 🕎 Dreidel
posts.excerpt:
Celebrate Hanukkah with a crystal dreidel that lives on your phone.

Tap to spin, watch it glow, and see where it lands – with modes for Israel and outside Israel.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
### What you’ll find inside:

- Realistic crystal dreidel with smooth animation
- Two game modes in Settings:
- Israel mode – letters like on dreidels in Israel
- Outside Israel mode – letters used in the diaspora
- Spin counter – see how long your dreidel spins and who wins
- Multiplayer mode – up to 8 players with coin tracking and up to 20 rounds
- Automatic Gimel / Hey / Nun / Shin–Pay outcomes using real dreidel rules
- Hebrew letters on every side
- Warm Hanukkah lights and menorah in the background
- Simple tap to spin controls – anyone can play

### Multiplayer Gameplay (new)

- Add up to 8 players
- Choose 1–20 rounds
- Everyone starts with coins and places 1 coin into the pot
- Each spin automatically applies the correct dreidel action
- The app tracks coins, pot, turns, and the final winner

### How to play:

- Open the app.
- (Optional) Go to Settings and choose Israel or Outside Israel mode.
- Tap the dreidel to spin it.
- Watch the counter and see whose dreidel spins longer.
- In multiplayer, see how the pot changes and who collects the most coins.

### Perfect for:

- Hanukkah gatherings and parties
- Playing dreidel when you don’t have a physical one
- Learning or revising the Hebrew dreidel letters in both traditions
- Playing full dreidel games with friends
- Adding cozy holiday vibes to your phone

*Light the candles, grab some chocolate coins (gelt), choose solo or multiplayer, and let the crystal dreidel spin – in Israel mode, outside-Israel mode, or both.*

posts.title: NeuroDream
posts.excerpt:
Vision board maker
NeuroDream is a modern vision board and manifestation app designed to help you organize your dreams, strengthen your mindset, and stay connected to your goals every day.

It combines classic visualization practices with neuroscience-inspired exercises to create a more active, structured, and motivating way to work toward the life you want.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
NeuroDream is a modern vision board and manifestation app designed to help you organize your dreams, strengthen your mindset, and stay connected to your goals every day.

It combines classic visualization practices with neuroscience-inspired exercises to create a more active, structured, and motivating way to work toward the life you want.

### With NeuroDream, you can:

- create dream lists and organize your goals
- add personal affirmations to each dream
- transform negative thoughts into positive affirmations
- mark goals as completed
- assign categories to your dreams
- use a Feng Shui-inspired board with life areas and categories
- create custom collage boards
- personalize your boards the way you want
- archive and share your boards
- add links to your dreams for extra inspiration and motivation
- set your dream boards as wallpapers
- use widgets to keep your goals visible every day
- set reminders for meditation and focus
- explore neuroplasticity-inspired exercises
- practice creative brain-training activities, including using your non-dominant hand

### **NeuroDream is made for people who want more than a traditional vision board. It gives you a personal space where dreams, affirmations, visualization, and daily practice come together in one app.
**

Whether your goals are about love, career, money, health, creativity, or personal growth, NeuroDream helps you keep them clear, visual, and close to your daily life.

Use dream boards, affirmations, wallpapers, links, and widgets to build a stronger connection to your future. Revisit your goals, stay inspired, and create a daily routine that supports your mindset and motivation.

NeuroDream is part of a wider ecosystem that also includes tools focused on calm, concentration, and brain training, helping you build a more intentional and focused lifestyle.

*Create your future with intention, clarity, and daily action.*

posts.title: Screen Time Monitor
posts.excerpt: Screen Time Monitor helps you understand how you spend time on your phone - by app and by day. View clear usage stats and graphs, then set notifications to stay on track.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
### **What you can do:**

- Daily screen time overview — see total device usage for today and recent days
- Per-app usage details — find the apps that take the most time
- Usage graphs — simple charts to spot patterns quickly
- Limit notifications — set a daily time limit for any app and get notified when you exceed it
- Lightweight and focused — no extra features, just monitoring and alerts

### **How it works**

- Open the app to see today’s screen time and top apps.
- Tap an app to view its daily usage.
- Set a limit (for example, 30 min/day).
- Get a notification when the limit is exceeded.


**Mood Tracker helps you understand how your apps affect your emotional state.
With Mood Tracker, you can select specific apps to track and see how they influence your mood over time. Simply mark the apps you want to monitor, and every time you open one of them, you will be asked to rate how you feel. When you leave the app, you’ll be prompted again.**

This creates a clear before-and-after picture of how each app impacts your mood — positively or negatively.

## **How it works:**
- Choose which apps you want to track using a simple checkbox list
- Get a short mood check-in when you open a tracked app
- Get another mood check-in when you leave it
- Review insights showing which apps improve your mood and which drain it

**By connecting screen time with emotional awareness, Mood Tracker helps you make healthier digital choices and regain control over how apps affect your well-being.**

*Made by Calmloop.*

posts.title: Reverse Audio Game
posts.excerpt: Turn speech into a hilarious challenge. Reverse Audio Game lets you flip any phrase backwards, practice saying the backward version, then play your attempt in the normal direction to hear how close you are to the original.
posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
### **How it works:**
🎤 Record a word or phrase
🔄 Play it backwards
🗣️ Practice & record yourself pronouncing the backward audio
↩️ Play your attempt in normal mode and compare with the original
💾 Save (download) or 📤 share your best takes

### **Why you’ll love it:**
⚡ Instant reverse & re-reverse playback
😂 Super simple flow for quick laughs with friends
💾 Save (download) & 📤 share recordings
✈️ Works offline — perfect for trips & parties

### **Privacy & permissions:**
🎙️ Microphone access for recording only
🔐 Recordings stay on your device unless you share them

*Ready to flip your voice and test your mimic skills? 🚀 Hit Record!*

posts.title: almloop

posts.author: The Cheervinsky Studio
posts.body:
Great for adults, kids, and anyone looking to stay mentally sharp.

### **🧘 ZEN MODE: SOUND + CALMING VISUALS**
